name: REAPER DAW MCP Server
what_it_does: REAPER DAW MCP server enabling AI agents to control REAPER digital audio workstation — managing tracks and items, controlling playback and recording, adjusting mixer levels and FX, and integrating REAPER's professional audio production capabilities into agent-driven music production and audio engineering workflows.
use_cases: Controlling REAPER playback and transport from AI music production agents
Managing tracks, items, and MIDI from automated composition agents
Adjusting mixer levels and effects parameters from audio engineering agents
Automating repetitive production tasks from workflow automation agents
Generating MIDI content and arranging tracks from AI composition agents
Batch processing audio files from post-production agents
not_for: Teams using Ableton, Pro Tools, or Logic (REAPER-specific; use Reascript for others)
Teams without REAPER license (requires REAPER installed locally)
Real-time performance control (latency from MCP layer may be unsuitable for live use)
best_when: A music production or audio engineering agent needs to control REAPER — for AI-assisted composition, automated audio processing, or workflow automation within REAPER's environment.
avoid_when: You're using a different DAW, need real-time performance control, or don't have REAPER installed.

auth.notes: No authentication — communicates with REAPER via ReaScript or REAPER's Web API. REAPER must be running locally. Enable REAPER Web API in preferences for network access.

pricing.notes: REAPER is inexpensive compared to other DAWs ($60 discounted license). 60-day fully functional trial. MCP server is free and open source.

performance.rate_limits: Local — limited by REAPER's API response time; no external limits

known_agent_gotchas: REAPER must be running with a project open before the MCP server can control it
REAPER Web API must be enabled in REAPER preferences for network access
Reascript API is REAPER-specific — agents need REAPER knowledge domain
MIDI and audio operations have real-time implications — test carefully
Community MCP — niche tool for audio professionals; limited contributor base
Project state is stateful — operations affect the REAPER project immediately
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local REAPER instance. No external credentials. Web API access should be localhost-only. Community MCP. No secrets needed. REAPER license required for full use beyond trial.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local REAPER instance. Localhost-only Web API. No secrets. Community MCP. REAPER license required.
